  • 08/07/26 Musselburgh--Sea Wall 50 (Lukáš Brezniak) "Drifting very far away from the shore, impossible to tell if there are any other scoter sp."
  • 08/07/26 Musselburgh Lagoons 1 (Vicky Yeung) "at least one individual seen with mixed scoter and eider group"
  • 08/07/26 Musselburgh Lagoons 1 (Owen Chiang) "at least one individual seen with mixed scoter and eider group"
  • 08/07/26 Musselburgh Sea Wall X (Sandeep Ramalingam) "Seen here a few days earlier here"
  • 08/07/26 Musselburgh--Esk Mouth X (Mark Easterbrook) "At least a dozen"
  • 08/07/26 Musselburgh--Sea Wall 50 (Stuart Read) "Mostly males"
  • 08/07/26 Musselburgh Lagoons X (Phil Bishop) "Minimum of 40, mostly drakes"
  • 08/07/26 Musselburgh Lagoons 45 (Nick Addey) "Summer flock. Mostly males."
  • 07/07/26 Musselburgh--Levenhall Links (General) 28 (Jill Williams) "Fabulous views swimming, feeding and preening"
  • 07/07/26 Musselburgh--Levenhall Links (General) 28 (Stu Elsom - Stuart Elsom Ecology / Rockjumper Birding Tours) "Fabulous views swimming, feeding and preening"
  • 07/07/26 Musselburgh--Sea Wall 60 (Harry Richardson) "From sea wall"
  • 07/07/26 Musselburgh--Levenhall Links (General) 60 (David Kelly) "Not rare here"
  • 07/07/26 Musselburgh Lagoons 40 (Phil Hyde) "At least 40, mainly males."
  • 07/07/26 Musselburgh Lagoons 61 (Matthew Mellor) "Lots of them!"
  • 07/07/26 Musselburgh Lagoons 60 (Muhummed H)
  • 07/07/26 Musselburgh--Sea Wall 60 (Noah Bailey) "Flock on sea"
  • 07/07/26 John Muir Way, Musselburgh GB-Scotland 55.95164, -3.02285 100 (Anthony Griffiths) "Mostly males in spread out flock from seawall"
  • 07/07/26 Musselburgh--Sea Wall 25 (Rich Bayldon)
  • 07/07/26 Musselburgh Lagoons 75 (Ray O'Reilly) "Mostly drakes"
  • 07/07/26 Musselburgh--Sea Wall 20 (Mike Barth) "Flocks with Common Scoter"

White-winged Scoter
More info
eBird
(Melanitta deglandi)

  • 10/07/26 Musselburgh--Levenhall Links (General) 2 (Sam Shutt) "Known immature males. Possibly third individual seen too (thought to be a 4cy or an adult, potentially the same one that was at Kirkcaldy) although glimpses were too fleeting to 100% confirm."
  • 10/07/26 Musselburgh--Levenhall Links (General) 1 (Tom Gale) "known bird (another also present, seen by other observer)"
  • 09/07/26 Musselburgh--Levenhall Links (General) 1 (David Kelly) "Continuing."
  • 08/07/26 Musselburgh Lagoons 1 (Robert Rackliffe) "Continuing bird associating with the Velvet Scoter flock although slightly distant. Good scope views."
  • 08/07/26 Musselburgh Lagoons 1 (Virginia Fairchild) "Continuing bird associating with the Velvet Scoter flock although slightly distant. Good scope views."
  • 08/07/26 Musselburgh--Esk Mouth 1 (Mark Easterbrook) "Still ptesent"
  • 08/07/26 Musselburgh Lagoons 1 (Phil Bishop) "Very brief view imm male. Small nasal bump, dark bill and pale not yellow cuttong edge that appeared pink. Dove as soon as got scope on it.."
  • 08/07/26 Musselburgh Lagoons 1 (Nick Addey) "3cy male with Velvet Scoter flock. IDd on bill profile shape, bump on bill obvious. Eye patch not fully developed."
  • 07/07/26 Musselburgh--Levenhall Links (General) 1 (David Kelly) "Bird first seen this morning, chased this afternoon."
  • 07/07/26 John Muir Way, Musselburgh GB-Scotland 55.95164, -3.02285 1 (Anthony Griffiths) "2cy Drake seen with 2commons videoed"
  • 07/07/26 Musselburgh--Sea Wall 1 (Rich Bayldon) "continuing 2s drake fairly close in offshore with 2 Common Scoter."
  • 07/07/26 Musselburgh Lagoons 1 (Ray O'Reilly) "Drake in mixed Scoter flock"
  • 07/07/26 Musselburgh--Sea Wall 1 (Mike Barth) "Well reported"

Goldeneye
More info
eBird
(Bucephala clangula)

  • 10/07/26 Musselburgh--Sea Wall 2 (Pasi Hyvönen) "Close sea wall"
  • 09/07/26 Musselburgh--Levenhall Links (General) 1 (David Kelly) "Continuing"
  • 09/07/26 Musselburgh--Sea Wall 2 (Lukáš Brezniak) "Pair flying by"
  • 09/07/26 Musselburgh--Sea Wall 2 (Eliška Malcová) "Pair flying by"

Swift
More info

  • 07/07/26 Musselburgh--Levenhall Links (General) 33 (David Kelly) "Large flocks of this species are typically brought down to the Scrapes by rain storms in July. Not rare here."
